summary: Account Dashboard + Subscription/Billing feature fully tested. Backend pytest: 10/10 PASS — public plan/config, /me/subscription defaults, trial start + re-use rejected, session cap (3) for free → 402 then unlimited after trial, monthly+annual checkout creates payment_transactions doc (status='initiated', amount/days correct), invalid plan → 400, password change (wrong current → 400, correct → re-login works), admin-only plan-prices (403 for non-admin, GET/PUT works and reflects in public config). Frontend: account-button on Dashboard navigates to AccountDashboard (plan-card visible), free user sees monthly $9.99 / annual $60 / 7-day trial buttons. start-trial-button activates trial, UI updates to 'Pro (Trial) · 7 days left'. Wrong current password shows 'Current password is incorrect'. upgrade-monthly-button calls POST /api/me/checkout with {plan:'monthly', origin_url} and redirects to Stripe (verified via route intercept). Free user gating: custom-freq-slider disabled, custom-freq-locked hint visible, ambient sliders disabled, Golden Stack and Breathwork toggles redirect to AccountDashboard. Admin user sees admin-prices-card with 3 inputs + save button — saving 11.49/69/10 reflects immediately in /api/plan/config, restored to defaults afterwards.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend /api/me/subscription days_left rounds UP via `delta.days + (1 if delta.seconds > 0 else 0)`. A trial started a few minutes ago shows 7 days_left even though it is actually ~6.99 days. Will show 1 day left when only 30 minutes remain — could mildly confuse users. Consider showing hours when days_left <= 1.

Backend _fulfill_payment extends from `current_until` if still in future. This lets a trial user pay for Pro and get 7-day trial + 30 days = ~37 days. May be intentional (free trial bonus stacks with paid) but worth a product decision; many SaaS replace trial with paid.

Backend /api/me/trial does not check email verification or any cool-down between accounts — same person can register multiple emails to get unlimited 7-day trials. Out of scope for MVP but worth flagging.

Frontend AccountDashboard useEffect dependency arrays are [] for both load() and the Stripe-return effect — fine for single-mount usage but won't re-fetch if `user` changes without remount. Acceptable since App.js remounts on view change.
Frontend AccountDashboard saveAdminPrices uses parseInt(trial) without radix; modern JS treats default as 10 so OK, but eslint typically warns.
Backend server.py at 611 lines is approaching the 700-line split threshold — consider splitting subscription/billing into a separate router module before adding more endpoints.

Frontend Dashboard.jsx: gating relies on isPro from SubscriptionContext; on first load isPro=false until /me/subscription resolves. A Pro user briefly sees locked UI for ~200ms after page load. Consider initial loading state to avoid flash.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of tested flows (account-dashboard nav, prices display, trial activation, password change err+success, checkout POST verified, free-user gating, admin prices update)

test_credentials: admin@example.com / admin123 (admin role, currently plan=basic, trial_used=false). Fresh users TEST_<tag>_<uuid>@example.com registered per test for clean state.
seed_data_creation: Each backend test registers a fresh TEST_ user. Test for session cap creates 4 TEST_cap_n sessions and 1 TEST_cap_pro after trial. Admin price-update test mutates plan_config but restores to defaults in a finally block.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_subscription.py — covers all 11 endpoints listed in the request. Stripe checkout call works against STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent and returns a real Stripe-hosted URL (do NOT navigate to it during UI testing; intercept the route or stop after the POST is captured). Admin@example.com's trial is still unused as of the end of this run — DO consume it explicitly in future trial tests, or register a fresh user. Plan_config was mutated and restored during admin tests; default values are monthly=9.99 / annual=60 / trial_days=7. The /api/plan/config endpoint is public (no auth needed).
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All 11 features in the request work end-to-end. Stripe redirect to checkout.stripe.com confirmed (URL pattern: https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_...).
